nodes.norm_label: owner action checklist (blocks public launch, not beta code)

nodes.label: Top issues ranked (50 collapsed to the 24 that matter)
nodes.norm_label: top issues ranked (50 collapsed to the 24 that matter)

nodes.label: This is NOT the Next.js you know
nodes.norm_label: this is not the next.js you know

nodes.label: Graphify Knowledge Graph Workflow
nodes.rationale: Query the scoped knowledge graph (query/path/explain) before grep or raw source browsing because it returns a much smaller scoped subgraph; update after code changes to keep the graph current at zero API cost.

nodes.label: Merai — AI Video Editing OS for Arabic Creators
nodes.rationale: Arabic-first product: upload raw footage, AI edits (transcription, best-take selection, silence/filler removal, captions), user reviews and exports.
nodes.norm_label: merai — ai video editing os for arabic creators

nodes.label: Merai Ground Rules (i18n strings, logical CSS, Haiku-only, signed URLs, no tracking)
nodes.rationale: Runtime AI limited to Claude Haiku as a margin constraint; no public storage buckets; every user-facing string externalized to messages/{ar,en}.json; logical CSS properties only for RTL correctness.
nodes.norm_label: merai ground rules (i18n strings, logical css, haiku-only, signed urls, no tracking)

nodes.label: Monorepo via npm workspaces
nodes.rationale: Plain npm workspaces, no Turborepo/Nx — a solo-scale build does not need build orchestration. Revisit when package count or build times grow.

nodes.label: Job queue: Postgres with FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED
nodes.rationale: No Redis/SQS: zero extra infra/cost, transactional with domain data, queryable status via read-only RLS. Exponential backoff retries (30s*2^attempts, max 3), dedupe_key for idempotent enqueues. Revisit above ~10 jobs/sec.
nodes.norm_label: job queue: postgres with for update skip locked

nodes.label: Enum-like columns as text + CHECK constraints
nodes.rationale: Adding a value to a CHECK is a cheap constraint swap; altering Postgres enums is more ceremony and historically hazardous in migrations.
nodes.norm_label: enum-like columns as text + check constraints

nodes.label: Timeline direction: LTR always, even in RTL UI
nodes.rationale: Media scrubbers flow left-to-right universally; mirroring would fight user muscle memory. Chrome is RTL-first but the timeline component is hard-pinned dir=ltr with time 0 at the left. Binding for Phase 3.
nodes.norm_label: timeline direction: ltr always, even in rtl ui

nodes.label: i18n: next-intl, Arabic default at root path
nodes.rationale: Arabic is the design target and served at / with no prefix; English adapts under /en. All strings in messages/{ar,en}.json; RTL via dir on html plus Tailwind logical utilities.
nodes.norm_label: i18n: next-intl, arabic default at root path

nodes.label: Browser locale detection disabled
nodes.rationale: Accept-Language detection would send English-configured browsers to /en, but Arabic creators commonly run English OS/browsers. / always serves Arabic; English is an explicit choice. Revisit when signup data shows English-primary usage.

nodes.label: Typography: IBM Plex Sans Arabic single family
nodes.rationale: One family covers Arabic and Latin so mixed-language lines never change font mid-sentence; also the default caption font in @merai/core caption specs.
nodes.norm_label: typography: ibm plex sans arabic single family

nodes.label: Retention windows: raw 30 days, exports 90 days
nodes.rationale: Raw files dominate storage cost at ~90% target margin; 30 days covers an edit cycle. EDL + transcript survive expiry so the edit is never lost — only re-rendering from raw becomes impossible. Constants RAW_RETENTION_DAYS/EXPORT_RETENTION_DAYS in @merai/core.
nodes.norm_label: retention windows: raw 30 days, exports 90 days

nodes.label: Worker runs TypeScript directly via tsx
nodes.rationale: @merai/core ships as TS source; web uses transpilePackages, worker executes with tsx at runtime — avoids a package build/watch pipeline for a solo team.
nodes.norm_label: worker runs typescript directly via tsx

nodes.label: EDL: versioned, immutable, append-only JSON (v1 single-track)
nodes.rationale: edl_versions has insert+select RLS only; every edit appends a version giving cheap undo/audit. v1 locks audio+video together; Phase 5 J/L-cuts will introduce v2 with decoupled tracks. Zod-validated on producer and consumer sides.
nodes.norm_label: edl: versioned, immutable, append-only json (v1 single-track)

nodes.label: Transcripts: normalized words + raw provider payload
nodes.rationale: transcripts.words is provider-agnostic for all downstream code; transcripts.raw keeps the full AssemblyAI response so analysis can be re-run without re-billing STT — the largest per-unit cost.
nodes.norm_label: transcripts: normalized words + raw provider payload

nodes.label: Auth: Supabase email+password
nodes.rationale: OAuth deferred until user pull. Locale-less /auth/confirm route handles token_hash and PKCE code; profile rows created by a DB trigger on auth.users insert carrying signup locale.

nodes.label: Next.js 16 conventions (proxy.ts, async params)
nodes.rationale: src/proxy.ts replaces middleware.ts (nodejs runtime); the proxy chains next-intl locale routing then Supabase session refresh on one response.

nodes.label: Transcription behind a provider interface; mock keyless default
nodes.rationale: AssemblyAIProvider and MockTranscriptionProvider share the SAME normalization code so mock tests exercise the production path. Env-only selection: ASSEMBLYAI_API_KEY presence activates the real provider. disfluencies:true keeps hesitation sounds for Phase 2 filler removal.
nodes.norm_label: transcription behind a provider interface; mock keyless default

nodes.label: 10-minute cap enforced in three layers, no ffprobe
nodes.rationale: Browser metadata probe (UX) + server action/DB CHECK + authoritative provider-measured audio_duration. Server-side ffprobe deliberately skipped — the STT provider gives the check for free. Spoofed durations caught only after bounded STT spend; acceptable at MVP scale.
nodes.norm_label: 10-minute cap enforced in three layers, no ffprobe

nodes.label: Pipeline status on projects.status; frontend polls
nodes.rationale: The job queue is an implementation detail; UI-facing state machine is projects.status (uploading→transcribing→analyzing→ready|error) polled every 2.5s. Supabase Realtime deliberately skipped — publication setup + reconnect handling for zero MVP benefit.
nodes.norm_label: pipeline status on projects.status; frontend polls

nodes.label: DB-level tests on PGlite applying real migrations
nodes.rationale: No Docker on the dev machine; PGlite (Postgres-in-WASM, in-process) applies the actual supabase/migrations/*.sql against stubbed auth/storage schemas and drives the real claim→handler→complete/fail path. Storage RLS and tus endpoint testable only on live Supabase.
nodes.norm_label: db-level tests on pglite applying real migrations

nodes.label: Resumable uploads: tus-js-client, 6 MiB chunks
nodes.rationale: Supabase Storage resumable protocol is tus 1.0.0 requiring 6 MiB chunks. Pause keeps the server session; auto-retry on dropped connections; fingerprint-based resume. Objects at {owner_id}/{upload_id}/original.<ext> in private raw-uploads bucket, uploaded with the user's JWT so storage RLS applies.

nodes.label: Arabic language handling: keep auto-detection, no pin
nodes.rationale: Live test: AssemblyAI auto-detected Arabic at 0.9984 confidence so source_language:auto stays. Caveats logged: hesitations can merge into the next word, brand names miss at high confidence, Arabic output arrived without punctuation despite punctuate:true — matters for captions.
nodes.norm_label: arabic language handling: keep auto-detection, no pin

nodes.label: Brand terms: custom_spelling adopted, word_boost rejected
nodes.rationale: Both probed live before adopting (lesson from the speech_models deprecation): word_boost changed nothing; custom_spelling fixed the token. Trade-off: force-mapping can overcorrect genuine uses; acceptable while recordings are product-adjacent.
nodes.norm_label: brand terms: custom_spelling adopted, word_boost rejected

nodes.label: Export: single-threaded ffmpeg.wasm core, self-hosted
nodes.rationale: Multithreaded core needs SharedArrayBuffer → COOP/COEP headers, and COEP would block cross-origin Supabase signed-URL media the editor plays. Single-thread renders slower but keeps the page unisolated. 32MB wasm core copied into public/ffmpeg at build — no CDN dependency.

nodes.label: Exported captions rasterized by the browser, never ffmpeg
nodes.rationale: ffmpeg drawtext without fribidi/harfbuzz renders Arabic disconnected and reversed; libass availability in wasm uncertain. Each caption line becomes a transparent full-frame PNG via Canvas2D (native Arabic shaping, same UI font) overlaid with enable=between(t,...) windows. Karaoke word-level burn stays preview-only in MVP.
nodes.norm_label: exported captions rasterized by the browser, never ffmpeg

nodes.label: Export renders SEGMENT-WISE; single filter graphs banned for cuts
nodes.rationale: Three single-command architectures OOMed even in native ffmpeg (per-caption overlays, N-branch trim+concat, single-pass select/aselect) — filter graphs buffer unboundedly on long inputs. Now: one input-seeked ffmpeg run per kept segment, captions as one concat-demuxer PNG sequence, final -c copy remux join. Peak memory bounded by one segment; native validation 36s on a 9.6-min clip.
nodes.norm_label: export renders segment-wise; single filter graphs banned for cuts

nodes.label: Export resolution: 720-class per aspect ratio
nodes.rationale: wasm encode speed and memory over pixels: 9:16→720x1280, 1:1→720x720, 16:9→1280x720; x264 veryfast + crf 23 + aac 128k. Revisit 1080p when the multithreaded-core question is answered.

nodes.label: Storage layout: owner-uuid-prefixed paths in private buckets
nodes.rationale: raw-uploads/{owner_id}/{upload_id}/original.<ext> and exports/{owner_id}/{export_id}.mp4; storage RLS grants access only when the first path folder equals auth.uid(). No public buckets — all delivery via signed URLs.
nodes.norm_label: storage layout: owner-uuid-prefixed paths in private buckets

nodes.label: Phase 2 — AI analysis layer (Haiku engine, EDL builder)
nodes.rationale: Cost rules: Haiku only, one forced tool-use JSON call per video, analysis persisted on the transcript so regeneration never re-bills. Keyless HeuristicAnalysisEngine fallback mirrors the transcription mock pattern. Measured live cost $0.0037 for a 24s video.
nodes.norm_label: phase 2 — ai analysis layer (haiku engine, edl builder)

nodes.label: Phase 3 — Review & text-based editing UI
nodes.rationale: One source of truth: the EDL. Every editor operation is a pure EdlV1→EdlV1 transform in @merai/core edl-ops; undo/redo is a snapshot stack; save appends an immutable version (source=user) preserving the AI draft forever. Every cut is explainable via a popover with the localized reason and the AI's note.

nodes.label: Phase 4 — Export pipeline, ffmpeg.wasm (live-verified)
nodes.rationale: Client-side render is a deliberate cost decision (PRD section 6). Pure export planning lives in a testable module; only the renderer touches ffmpeg. Export flow: auto-save dirty EDL → exports row 'rendering' → signed-URL download → render with progress → upload to exports bucket → 'uploaded'.

nodes.label: Phase 4 — 10-minute export stress test (resolved segment-wise)
nodes.rationale: Browser single-thread wasm render completed with no OOM at ~1.8x realtime; the failure moved to upload — the 53.7MB output exceeds Supabase free-tier 50MB per-file cap, biting at roughly the 9-10-min ceiling. Follow-ups: keep local download when cloud upload fails; decide paid-tier limit raise vs. free-tier bitrate ceiling.

nodes.label: Next.js 16 breaking-changes agent rule (read bundled docs first)
nodes.norm_label: next.js 16 breaking-changes agent rule (read bundled docs first)
